scope: Defines the resolution matrix for each execution exit blocker type. Specifies resolution paths, required upstream fields, auto-unblock rules, prohibited behaviors, and recommended implementation order. This contract codifies what must happen before any blocker can be lifted — it does not implement any resolution logic.

terminal_event_unpriced.description: A terminal event (e.g. delist) is recorded but no pricing layer in the terminal_exit_pricing_hierarchy has been applied. actual_exit_date and actual_sell_price remain NULL.

resolution_path.description: Apply the terminal_exit_pricing_hierarchy (cash_settlement -> last_tradable_close -> zero_recovery) in the upstream execution path to resolve pricing for each terminal event.
resolution_path.resolution_criteria: Row transitions from terminal_event_unpriced to one of the terminal_priced_* states when actual_exit_date, actual_sell_price, and execution_delayed_realized_return are all populated from an auditable pricing layer.
resolution_path.auto_unblock_condition: Only when upstream execution path formally outputs a terminal_priced_* state with complete required portfolio fields.
resolution_path.fallback_if_unresolvable: Remain hard_blocker=true. Must not be silently dropped or filtered.

prohibited_workarounds: Silently apply zero_recovery without policy decision
Filter rows and continue portfolio construction

exit_unresolved.description: No exit signal and no terminal event is recorded. The exit path is entirely unresolved. actual_exit_date is NULL.

resolution_path.description: Complete the execution path by determining whether the exit is a normal D5 close, a delayed sell, or a missing terminal event record. If the exit is truly unresolvable (no tradable path, no terminal event), it remains a hard blocker.
resolution_path.resolution_criteria: Row transitions from exit_unresolved to a resolved state when actual_exit_date, actual_sell_price, and execution_delayed_realized_return are all populated from the upstream execution path. This may require calendar/tradability source investigation if the exit path was never computed.
resolution_path.auto_unblock_condition: Only when upstream execution path formally outputs complete actual exit fields with a valid execution_path_status.
resolution_path.fallback_if_unresolvable: Remain hard_blocker=true. Must not be silently dropped or filtered.

prohibited_workarounds: Assume normal D5 exit without evidence
Filter rows and continue portfolio construction

calendar_insufficient.description: Calendar data is insufficient to determine the exit date or to verify tradability windows.

resolution_path.description: Repair or extend the trading calendar and tradability source coverage so that exit timing and tradability can be determined for all affected instruments and dates.
resolution_path.resolution_criteria: Row transitions from calendar_insufficient when trading calendar and tradability data are sufficient to determine exit date and verify tradability for the affected instrument-date pairs.
resolution_path.auto_unblock_condition: Only when upstream calendar/tradability source is repaired and the execution path recomputes exit timing.
resolution_path.fallback_if_unresolvable: Remain hard_blocker=true. Must not be silently dropped or filtered.

prohibited_workarounds: Skip tradability check for exit date
Filter rows and continue portfolio construction

recommended_implementation_order.action: Implement last_tradable_close auditability check for terminal_event_unpriced rows
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: All 10 terminal_event_unpriced rows have last_tradable_date available but the terminal_event source is contract_degraded. Verifying that auditable close prices exist for those last-tradable dates is the lowest-risk path to reducing the blocker count.
recommended_implementation_order.expected_effect: If auditable close prices are confirmed, these 10 rows can transition to terminal_priced_last_tradable_close.

recommended_implementation_order.action: Decide whether zero_recovery is enabled as a formal policy
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: If last_tradable_close audit fails for any row, zero_recovery is the only remaining pricing layer. This must be an explicit policy decision with terminal_exit_conservative_flag=true, not a default fallback.
recommended_implementation_order.expected_effect: Rows that fail last_tradable_close audit could be priced at zero_recovery, producing conservative but complete portfolio entries.

recommended_implementation_order.action: Investigate exit_unresolved rows for calendar/source coverage gaps
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: The 10 exit_unresolved rows may stem from calendar gaps, tradability source gaps, or genuinely missing exit signals. Diagnosis should distinguish these cases before any resolution is attempted.
recommended_implementation_order.expected_effect: Identifies whether exit_unresolved rows are fixable via source repair or are permanently unresolvable.

recommended_implementation_order.action: Restore portfolio dry-run after all resolvable blockers are cleared
recommended_implementation_order.rationale: Portfolio dry-run must fail-fast on any remaining hard blockers. It should only be re-enabled when zero rows with actual_exit_date=NULL exist in the backtest_executable subset.
recommended_implementation_order.expected_effect: Clean portfolio dry-run with all exit paths fully resolved.

notes: This contract defines resolution requirements only. It does not implement any resolution logic, pricing, or filtering.
All three blocker types default to hard_blocker=true. They can only be unblocked by upstream resolution producing complete actual_exit_date, actual_sell_price, and execution_delayed_realized_return.
Portfolio layer must never resolve, filter, or work around any blocker type.
planned_exit_date substitution is prohibited under all circumstances.
silent zero_recovery (zero_recovery applied without terminal_exit_conservative_flag=true and formal policy decision) is prohibited.
